title: Giant Unilamellar Vesicles (GUVs) Preparation by Electroformation Method
author: Zehra Kahveci
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rvvd666

steps.title: Weigh phospholipid of interest, dissolve the...
steps.instruction: Weigh phospholipid of interest, dissolve the powder in chloroform at 10 mM final concentration.

steps.title: For fluorescent GUVs, add fluorescent phospholipid...
steps.instruction: For fluorescent GUVs, add fluorescent phospholipid Texas-Red-DHPE (1 mM stock) to reach the final molar ratio of 1:500.

steps.title: Identify the conductive sides of the ITO-slides with a multimeter
steps.instruction: Identify the conductive sides of the ITO-slides with a multimeter.

steps.title: Use a 5 μl Hamilton syringe...
steps.instruction: Use a 5 μl Hamilton syringe to spread 10 μl of phospholipid stock solution on one of the ITO glass slide.

steps.title: Leave the ITO coating side upwards...
steps.instruction: Leave the ITO coating side upwards into a vacuum dessicator for 15 min.

steps.title: Prepare 195 mM sucrose solution, dissolve...
steps.instruction: Prepare 195 mM sucrose solution, dissolve it in Hepes Buffer 5 mM pH 7.4, vortex until sucrose is dissolved.

steps.title: Spread a thin layer of vacuum...
steps.instruction: Spread a thin layer of vacuum grease evenly on the O-ring that you use.

steps.title: After the ITO-slides are dried, take...
steps.instruction: After the ITO-slides are dried, take the top part off from the Nanion Vesicle Prep Pro chamber by removing the screws.

steps.title: Use tweezers to place the ITO-slide...
steps.instruction: Use tweezers to place the ITO-slide lipid film point upwards in the Nanion Vesicle Prep Pro.

steps.title: Place an O-ring around the dried...
steps.instruction: Place an O-ring around the dried lipid film, add 280 μl fof 195 mM sucrose solution into the O-ring.

steps.title: Place a second ITO-slide on top...
steps.instruction: Place a second ITO-slide on top of the O-ring with the conductive side facing downwards using tweezers. Note that, the right end of the top slide has to touch properly to the electrode on the right-side.

steps.title: Place the top part of the...
steps.instruction: Place the top part of the chamber back on and screw it carefully.

steps.title: Turn-on the Nanion VPP, select the...
steps.instruction: Turn-on the Nanion VPP, select the appropriate protocol and initiate the formation process. 00:05:00, Frequency 5 Hz, applied voltage from 0 to 3 V 02:00:00, Frequency 5 Hz, applied voltage 3 V 00:05:00, Frequency 5 Hz, applied voltage from 3V to 0 Undetermined

steps.title: After the protocol ends remove the...
steps.instruction: After the protocol ends remove the screws and take off the top part of the chamber. Use the tweezers to remove the top ITO-slide.

steps.title: Cut a pipette tip and collect...
steps.instruction: Cut a pipette tip and collect the sample from the ITO-slide surface slowly.

steps.title: Withdraw a 5 µl aliquot of...
steps.instruction: Withdraw a 5 µl aliquot of the electroformation product and inspect the GUVs under an epifluorescence microscope.
